content.text: A feature risk register is a structured document that catalogues every identified risk associated with a GenAI feature release, assesses each risk's likelihood and impact, assigns a mitigation action and owner, and tracks the risk's status through the launch lifecycle. Unlike the FMEA worksheet (which focuses on model failure modes within the conversational flow), the risk register covers the full release scope: technical, operational, legal, financial, and reputational risks.

attrs.alt: GenAI Feature Risk Heat Map — Likelihood vs. Impact

content.text: Why GenAI Features Need a Dedicated Risk Register

Standard software release risk registers cover deployment failures, rollback plans, and service degradation. GenAI features introduce a category of risks that do not exist in conventional software: risks that are probabilistic rather than deterministic, risks that compound over time (a model that drifts subtly), and risks that are societal as well as technical (a feature that disproportionately affects vulnerable users). A GenAI risk register must account for all of these.
Conventional registers assume deterministic outputs — same input, same output
Vulnerable user harmed, jailbreak succeeds, PII leaked in response
Conventional registers focus on data security, not output-level harm
EU AI Act classification, GDPR consent gap, HIPAA PHI in prompt
Conventional registers rarely include AI-specific regulatory frameworks
Token cost spike, provider rate limit, model deprecation
Conventional registers do not account for variable-cost, third-party-hosted computation
Viral screenshot of harmful output, trust collapse after hallucination
Conventional registers treat reputation as a business outcome, not a technical risk to design for
Each row of the risk register is a RiskEntry. The template covers all fields required for a GenAI release.

from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional


class RiskCategory(Enum):
    MODEL_QUALITY  = "model_quality"
    SAFETY_HARM    = "safety_harm"
    REGULATORY     = "regulatory"
    OPERATIONAL    = "operational"
    REPUTATIONAL   = "reputational"
    FINANCIAL      = "financial"


class Likelihood(Enum):
    RARE           = 1
    UNLIKELY       = 2
    POSSIBLE       = 3
    LIKELY         = 4
    ALMOST_CERTAIN = 5


class Impact(Enum):
    NEGLIGIBLE = 1
    LOW        = 2
    MEDIUM     = 3
    HIGH       = 4
    CRITICAL   = 5


class RiskStatus(Enum):
    OPEN       = "open"
    MITIGATED  = "mitigated"   # mitigation in place; risk still exists but reduced
    ACCEPTED   = "accepted"    # team has consciously accepted the residual risk
    CLOSED     = "closed"      # risk no longer applies (feature scoped out, etc.)
    TRIGGERED  = "triggered"   # risk has materialised and is being managed as incident


@dataclass
class RiskEntry:
    risk_id: str                       # e.g. "R1"
    title: str
    description: str
    category: RiskCategory
    likelihood: Likelihood
    impact: Impact
    mitigation: str                    # specific action taken or planned
    mitigation_owner: str              # role or person accountable for mitigation
    residual_likelihood: Likelihood    # after mitigation is applied
    residual_impact: Impact
    status: RiskStatus = RiskStatus.OPEN
    trigger_condition: str = ""        # what event would cause this risk to materialise
    contingency: str = ""              # what to do if it triggers despite mitigation
    review_date: Optional[str] = None  # next scheduled review (ISO-8601)

    @property
    def raw_score(self) -> int:
        return self.likelihood.value * self.impact.value

    @property
    def residual_score(self) -> int:
        return self.residual_likelihood.value * self.residual_impact.value

    @property
    def is_release_blocking(self) -> bool:
        """Risk blocks release if residual impact is CRITICAL or raw score ≥ 20."""
        return (
            self.residual_impact == Impact.CRITICAL
            or self.raw_score >= 20
        ) and self.status == RiskStatus.OPEN

    def risk_level(self) -> str:
        score = self.residual_score
        if score >= 15:  return "CRITICAL"
        if score >= 9:   return "HIGH"
        if score >= 4:   return "MEDIUM"
        return "LOW"
Standard Risk Entries for GenAI Releases
The following six risk entries appear in almost every GenAI feature risk register. They are the starting point; the team adds feature-specific risks on top.

R1 = RiskEntry(
    risk_id="R1",
    title="Hallucination in high-stakes output",
    description=(
        "The model produces a plausible-sounding but incorrect answer in a context "
        "where the user will act on it (medical, financial, legal, operational domain). "
        "The user does not realise the output is wrong."
    ),
    category=RiskCategory.MODEL_QUALITY,
    likelihood=Likelihood.LIKELY,
    impact=Impact.HIGH,
    mitigation=(
        "1. Confidence threshold: outputs below 0.7 are shown with a low-confidence "
        "disclaimer and a 'verify with a professional' note. "
        "2. Citation required: factual claims must cite a source. "
        "3. Eval gate: hallucination rate ≤ 5% on domain eval dataset."
    ),
    mitigation_owner="AI Safety Lead",
    residual_likelihood=Likelihood.POSSIBLE,
    residual_impact=Impact.MEDIUM,
    trigger_condition="User asks a factual question outside the model's training distribution",
    contingency="HITL escalation available; feature includes 'I may be wrong' footer",
    review_date="2025-08-01",
)

R2 = RiskEntry(
    risk_id="R2",
    title="Refusal policy miss — adversarial prompt bypasses guardrail",
    description=(
        "A user (or automated attack) crafts a prompt that causes the model to produce "
        "output that the refusal policy should block: harmful content, competitor information, "
        "or data outside scope."
    ),
    category=RiskCategory.SAFETY_HARM,
    likelihood=Likelihood.POSSIBLE,
    impact=Impact.CRITICAL,
    mitigation=(
        "1. Adversarial eval suite: 50+ jailbreak patterns tested at every release. "
        "2. Input guardrail: topic classifier blocks known out-of-scope categories before LLM call. "
        "3. Output guardrail: content filter on response before rendering."
    ),
    mitigation_owner="AI Safety Lead",
    residual_likelihood=Likelihood.UNLIKELY,
    residual_impact=Impact.HIGH,
    trigger_condition="Novel jailbreak pattern not in eval suite",
    contingency="Output monitoring alert → manual review → prompt patch within 4h",
    review_date="2025-08-01",
)

The risk register is a living document — not a one-time exercise at the requirements phase. It must be reviewed at four mandatory checkpoints and updated whenever a new risk is identified or a mitigation changes.
Full register populated; all R1–R6 baselines set
All SAFETY_HARM and REGULATORY risks reviewed; residual scores confirmed
All open risks re-assessed; release-blocking check run
Triggered risks reviewed; new operational risks added from incident log
Incident response; risk status → TRIGGERED; contingency executed

The six standard entries (R1–R6) are always present, but a complete register adds feature-specific risks. The following code shows how to build the register from all six entries and print a release-readiness summary.

register = RiskRegister(
    feature_id="FEAT-2024-045-medical-summary",
    system_prompt_version="v1.3.2",
    last_reviewed_at="2025-07-15",
    reviewed_by="Alice Chen (AI Safety Lead), Bob Park (Prod Owner)",
)

# R3: Token cost exceeds monthly budget
R3 = RiskEntry(
    risk_id="R3",
    title="Token cost exceeds monthly budget",
    description=(
        "At p99 prompt length the cost per request exceeds budget projections. "
        "Viral or bot-driven usage could push monthly spend 3x over forecast."
    ),
    category=RiskCategory.FINANCIAL,
    likelihood=Likelihood.LIKELY,
    impact=Impact.MEDIUM,
    mitigation=(
        "1. Per-request token cap: 2,048 output tokens hard limit. "
        "2. Monthly budget alert at 70% of cap. "
        "3. Request rate limiting per user."
    ),
    mitigation_owner="Engineering Lead",
    residual_likelihood=Likelihood.UNLIKELY,
    residual_impact=Impact.LOW,
    trigger_condition="Monthly spend exceeds $10,000",
    contingency="Automatic model downgrade to cheaper tier; alert on-call",
)

# R4: PII included in prompt (data breach)
R4 = RiskEntry(
    risk_id="R4",
    title="PII included in prompt — data breach",
    description=(
        "User or system injects personally identifiable information into the prompt. "
        "The LLM provider's data retention policy may persist or log this information."
    ),
    category=RiskCategory.REGULATORY,
    likelihood=Likelihood.UNLIKELY,
    impact=Impact.CRITICAL,
    mitigation=(
        "1. PII detector in input guardrail (regex + ML classifier for email, SSN, DOB). "
        "2. Data processing agreement with LLM provider. "
        "3. Zero-retention API mode enabled."
    ),
    mitigation_owner="Legal Counsel + Engineering Lead",
    residual_likelihood=Likelihood.RARE,
    residual_impact=Impact.HIGH,
    trigger_condition="PII classifier fires on user input or system-assembled prompt",
    contingency="Block request; log incident; notify DPO within 24h",
)

# R5: Latency SLO miss
R5 = RiskEntry(
    risk_id="R5",
    title="Inference latency exceeds p95 SLO",
    description=(
        "The LLM API call takes longer than the 2,500ms p95 target under normal load. "
        "At peak load (market open, 09:00–09:30) this may become p50."
    ),
    category=RiskCategory.OPERATIONAL,
    likelihood=Likelihood.POSSIBLE,
    impact=Impact.HIGH,
    mitigation=(
        "1. Streaming output: first token visible within 300ms. "
        "2. Load test at 2x expected peak; SLO verified before launch. "
        "3. Model caching for deterministic prompts."
    ),
    mitigation_owner="Engineering Lead",
    residual_likelihood=Likelihood.UNLIKELY,
    residual_impact=Impact.MEDIUM,
    trigger_condition="p95 latency alert fires (threshold: 3,000ms)",
    contingency="Fallback to cached last-known-good response; degrade gracefully",
)

# R6: Vendor API deprecation / lock-in
R6 = RiskEntry(
    risk_id="R6",
    title="Vendor API deprecation / lock-in",
    description=(
        "The LLM provider deprecates the model version or raises pricing substantially. "
        "Without an abstraction layer, migration requires re-writing all prompt logic."
    ),
    category=RiskCategory.OPERATIONAL,
    likelihood=Likelihood.UNLIKELY,
    impact=Impact.LOW,
    mitigation=(
        "1. LLM abstraction layer (provider-agnostic interface). "
        "2. Secondary provider tested quarterly. "
        "3. Prompt compatibility test suite runs against both providers."
    ),
    mitigation_owner="Engineering Lead",
    residual_likelihood=Likelihood.RARE,
    residual_impact=Impact.LOW,
    trigger_condition="Provider announces deprecation or >20% pricing increase",
    contingency="Switch to secondary provider; run compat tests; deploy within 48h",
)

for entry in [R1, R2, R3, R4, R5, R6]:
    register.add(entry)

print(register.summary())
# {
#   'total': 6,
#   'open': 6,
#   'release_blocking': 0,       # after mitigations, no entry is blocking
#   'is_release_ready': True,
#   'open_high_or_critical': ['R1', 'R2']  # still require monitoring
# }

When a risk transitions to TRIGGERED status — meaning it has materialised in production — the register drives a structured escalation sequence. This prevents ad-hoc incident response and ensures the contingency plan documented at authoring time is actually executed.

from enum import Enum, auto


class EscalationTier(Enum):
    P0 = auto()   # total outage or CRITICAL safety incident
    P1 = auto()   # partial outage or HIGH risk triggered
    P2 = auto()   # degraded performance or MEDIUM risk triggered
    P3 = auto()   # monitoring alert only; no user impact


ESCALATION_MAP: dict[str, EscalationTier] = {
    "CRITICAL": EscalationTier.P0,
    "HIGH":     EscalationTier.P1,
    "MEDIUM":   EscalationTier.P2,
    "LOW":      EscalationTier.P3,
}

# SLA in minutes: time from trigger detection to first response
ESCALATION_SLA: dict[EscalationTier, int] = {
    EscalationTier.P0: 5,
    EscalationTier.P1: 30,
    EscalationTier.P2: 120,
    EscalationTier.P3: 480,
}


def trigger_risk(register: RiskRegister, risk_id: str) -> None:
    entry = next(e for e in register.entries if e.risk_id == risk_id)
    entry.status = RiskStatus.TRIGGERED

    tier = ESCALATION_MAP[entry.risk_level()]
    sla_minutes = ESCALATION_SLA[tier]

    print(f"[RISK TRIGGERED] {entry.risk_id}: {entry.title}")
    print(f"  Level:       {entry.risk_level()} -> {tier.name}")
    print(f"  SLA:         {sla_minutes}min to first response")
    print(f"  Contingency: {entry.contingency}")
    print(f"  Owner:       {entry.mitigation_owner}")

    if tier in (EscalationTier.P0, EscalationTier.P1):
        _page_oncall(entry)
        _open_incident_ticket(entry, tier)


def _page_oncall(entry: RiskEntry) -> None:
    # Integrates with PagerDuty or OpsGenie
    print(f"  >> Paging on-call: {entry.mitigation_owner}")


def _open_incident_ticket(entry: RiskEntry, tier: EscalationTier) -> None:
    print(f"  >> Opening {tier.name} incident in Jira/Linear")

The six standard entries are a starting point. Every GenAI feature has its own risk profile depending on domain, user population, and system prompt scope. Use the checklist below during the requirements phase to identify feature-specific risks to add to the register.
Model provides incorrect dosage, diagnosis, or contraindication advice
Model generates specific investment advice without required disclaimers
Output inappropriate for age group slips through content filter
Generated code contains security vulnerability (SQL injection, path traversal)
Model makes unauthorised commitments (refunds, SLAs) on behalf of the company
Retrieval returns stale or retracted documents; model presents them as current
Language-detection error routes query to wrong knowledge base; wrong-language response
For each identified risk, apply the same

# Example: adding a feature-specific risk (medical domain)
R7 = RiskEntry(
    risk_id="R7",
    title="Model recommends incorrect medication dosage",
    description=(
        "For the medication lookup feature, the model may hallucinate a dosage that "
        "differs from official prescribing information. A patient acting on this "
        "could be harmed."
    ),
    category=RiskCategory.SAFETY_HARM,
    likelihood=Likelihood.POSSIBLE,
    impact=Impact.CRITICAL,
    mitigation=(
        "1. Ground all medication answers to curated drug database (RAG, not parametric memory). "
        "2. Append legal disclaimer: 'Verify with a pharmacist or prescriber.' "
        "3. Eval gate: 0% error rate on controlled medication dataset (200 cases)."
    ),
    mitigation_owner="AI Safety Lead + Medical SME",
    residual_likelihood=Likelihood.UNLIKELY,
    residual_impact=Impact.HIGH,
    trigger_condition="User reports incorrect dosage or clinical SME flags wrong answer in review",
    contingency="Disable medication lookup feature; switch to static reference redirect",
    review_date="2025-08-01",
)
# R7 residual: UNLIKELY × HIGH = 2×4 = 8  -> HIGH, not release-blocking if OPEN
# but given medical domain, team may choose to accept only MITIGATED status at launch
register.add(R7)

A GenAI feature risk register extends the conventional release risk register with categories that do not appear in standard software: model quality risks (hallucination, calibration), safety and harm risks (jailbreak, vulnerable user, PII leakage), regulatory risks (EU AI Act, GDPR, HIPAA), and reputational risks (viral harmful output). Each entry uses a 5×5 likelihood × impact matrix to produce a raw score and a residual score after mitigation. Risks with a residual impact of CRITICAL or a raw score ≥ 20 block the release unless their status is MITIGATED or ACCEPTED. The register is reviewed at Gate 1, Gate 2, one week before launch, and 30 days post-launch.
: risk_id, title, description, category, likelihood × impact, mitigation, owner, residual scores, status, trigger condition, contingency
: residual_impact == CRITICAL or raw_score ≥ 20 with status OPEN — must resolve before launch
: R1 hallucination, R2 refusal miss, R3 cost overrun, R4 PII in prompt, R5 latency SLO miss, R6 vendor lock-in — present in every GenAI register
: Gate 1, Gate 2, pre-launch, 30-day post-launch, and whenever a risk triggers in production
: returns False if any entry is release-blocking — the launch gate check
